title: Auto-derive inputSchema from example_minimal; populate ToolRegistration.json_schema
description:
Today ToolRegistration.json_schema is declared on the dataclass but never populated by any register() call (audit confirmed in plan-mode Phase 1). Without it, the stdio MCP server's tools/list cannot emit proper inputSchema per tool, and external agents can't render args.

Critical files:
- src/trade_trace/contracts/json_schema_derive.py (new) — pure function derive_schema(example: dict) -> dict that walks a payload, infers types per key (str→string, int→integer, float→number, bool→boolean, list→array (items recurse), dict→object (properties recurse)), and returns a valid JSON Schema dict with type=object and required-keys list.
- src/trade_trace/contracts/tool_registry.py::ToolRegistry.register (lines 121-148) — after the existing CLI-collision check, if json_schema is None and example_minimal is non-null, derive_schema(example_minimal) and store. Explicit json_schema= kwarg wins over the derived one.
- src/trade_trace/tools/journal.py::_tool_schema (lines 213-284) — already echoes reg.json_schema; no code change needed.

Reuses:
- src/trade_trace/tools/_examples.py::WRITE_TOOL_EXAMPLES — the source of example_minimal payloads (7 core write tools today).
- src/trade_trace/contracts/report_filter.py::ReportFilter.model_json_schema — for tools that accept a 'filter' arg, the schema points at the canonical ReportFilter schema instead of deriving.

Idempotency keys + dry-run flags + confirm: derive_schema must NOT include the underscore-prefixed transport keys (_dry_run, _confirm, _allow_no_idempotency) as required fields, but should document them in the schema's description.
acceptance_criteria: After build_registry() every tool with example_minimal has a non-null reg.json_schema. The tool.schema envelope's json_schema field is a valid JSON Schema dict (validate against the JSON Schema 2020-12 meta-schema or equivalent). Explicit json_schema= overrides derivation. ≥6 named tests: per-primitive derivation (str/int/float/bool); nested-object handling; list of objects; tools without example_minimal leave json_schema=null; explicit override wins; ReportFilter-typed filter args land the canonical schema.

dependents.title: Test QC: agent-ready surface (parity, smoke, schema audit, embeddings)
dependents.description:
Verify the agent-ready epic with ≥2 tests per capability area. Same pattern as the prior milestone Test QC beads (d0w, 5n4, 722).

Capability areas + acceptance:
- stdio MCP handshake: ≥2 tests covering initialize + capability negotiation.
- tools/list schema audit: ≥2 tests — every tool has a non-null inputSchema; the schema validates against the JSON Schema 2020-12 meta-schema.
- tools/call round-trip parity vs mcp_call: ≥2 tests covering a representative read tool + representative write tool with dry-run.
- Embeddings off-by-default invariant (regression-proof against sqlite-vec wiring): ≥2 tests — fresh init never loads the extension, journal.status reports provider='none'.
- Semantic recall returns provenance: ≥2 tests — with provider switched, memory.recall items carry the semantic strategy in strategy_provenance and the API key/path never appears in any returned field.
- Model import air-gap: ≥1 test — tt model import succeeds with socket.socket patched to raise.
- Keyring isolation: ≥1 test — provider switch round-trip with no plaintext key in DB / config / outbox JSONL.

Close reason must cite the exact test names per capability.
dependents.acceptance_criteria: ≥6 capability areas with ≥2 named tests each (≥12 total). Per-capability test names cited in close reason. False-green guard: any uncovered capability triggers a blocking fix bead before this QC closes.

dependents.title: CI smoke test: stdio server subprocess + tools/list + tools/call round-trip
dependents.description:
A fast integration test that spawns the real stdio MCP server as a subprocess, sends a few JSON-RPC frames over stdin, and confirms the protocol works end-to-end. Catches regressions that the in-process mcp_call shim cannot.

Critical files:
- tests/integration/test_mcp_stdio_server.py (new) — subprocess spawn (sys.executable + entry point or python -m trade_trace.mcp_server), send JSON-RPC frames over stdin, parse frames from stdout, assert protocol-level invariants.

Coverage matrix:
- Process boots without error.
- JSON-RPC initialize round-trips (server returns serverInfo + capabilities).
- tools/list returns ≥30 entries; each entry has name + description + inputSchema (validate the schema is a dict with type='object').
- tools/call round-trip for a representative READ tool (report.filter_schema) — result data equals mcp_call('report.filter_schema', {}).data.
- tools/call round-trip for a representative WRITE tool in dry-run mode (venue.add with _dry_run=true) — confirms no persisted row.
dependents.acceptance_criteria: ≥4 named tests run as part of pytest -q with the [mcp] extra installed. Runtime <10s (subprocess cold-start is the dominant cost). Skipped (not failed) when the mcp SDK is missing — the [mcp] extra is optional.

dependents.title: Docs QC: PRD MCP transport + AGENT_GUIDE.md truthfulness
dependents.description:
Audit shipped docs against the agent-ready impl. Same pattern as the prior milestone Docs QC beads (49v, wq0, 7v6, 89f).

Files to audit + per-file disposition in close reason:
- PRD.md §2.3 (CLI/MCP parity claim) — confirm or update with the now-shipped stdio transport.
- docs/architecture/contracts.md §1-§2 (MCP name mapping + parity) — note that schemas now ship via auto-derivation; cross-link to the new AGENT_GUIDE.md.
- docs/architecture/memory-layer.md §8 (Embeddings) — flip the 'deferred to a4p' banners to 'shipped in agent-ready epic'.
- README.md — confirm install + status section reflects M0-M4 PLUS agent-ready milestone.
- docs/AGENT_GUIDE.md — exists, ≤800 lines, references real tools.
- docs/CLAUDE_DESKTOP.md, CLAUDE_CODE.md, IDE_MCP_SETUP.md — each carries a working snippet.
- docs/architecture/security.md §3 (Model-host phishing row) — confirm matches the shipped API-provider flow.

Boundary cross-check: shipped CLI/MCP surface in README must equal the tool registry; no advertised tool returns UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY without an explicit disclaimer.
dependents.acceptance_criteria: Per-file disposition recorded in close reason: {edit-required: yes|no} plus the exact section/line that confirms accuracy. P1/P2/deferred markers cross-checked. No code/docs drift triggering a new blocking fix bead.
